cartons.name: bhm_aqmes_med
cartons.summary: Spectroscopically confirmed optically bright SDSS QSOs, selected from the SDSS QSO catalog (DR16Q, Lyke et al., 2020). Located in 36 mostly disjoint fields within the SDSS QSO footprint that were pre-selected to contain higher than average numbers of bright QSOs and CSC targets. The list of field centres can be found within the target_selection repository.
cartons.selection: Select all objects from SDSS DR16 QSO catalog that have 16.0<<tt>sdss_psfmag_i</tt><19.1 AB, that lie within 1.49 degrees of at least one AQMES-medium field location.

cartons.name: bhm_aqmes_med_faint
cartons.summary: Spectroscopically confirmed optically faint SDSS QSOs, selected from the SDSS QSO catalog (DR16Q, Lyke et al., 2020). Located in 36 mostly disjoint fields within the SDSS QSO footprint that were pre-selected to contain higher than average numbers of bright QSOs and CSC targets. The list of field centres can be found within the target_selection repository.
cartons.selection: Select all objects from SDSS DR16 QSO catalog that have 19.1<<tt>sdss_psfmag_i</tt><21.0 AB, that lie within 1.49 degrees of at least one AQMES-medium field location.

cartons.name: bhm_aqmes_wide2
cartons.summary: Spectroscopically confirmed optically bright SDSS QSOs, selected from the SDSS QSO catalog (DR16Q, Lyke et al., 2020). Located in 425 fields within the SDSS QSO footprint, where the choice of survey area prioritized field that overlapped with the SPIDERS footprint (approx 180<b<360 deg), and/or had higher than average numbers of bright QSOs and CSC targets. The list of field centres can be found within the target_selection repository.
cartons.selection: Select all objects from SDSS DR16 QSO catalog that have 16.0<<tt>sdss_psfmag_i</tt><19.1 AB, and that lie within 1.49 degrees of at least one AQMES-wide field location.

cartons.name: bhm_aqmes_wide2_faint
cartons.summary: Spectroscopically confirmed optically faint SDSS QSOs, selected from the SDSS QSO catalog (DR16Q, Lyke et al., 2020). Located in 425 fields within the SDSS QSO footprint, where the choice of survey area prioritized field that overlapped with the SPIDERS footprint (approx 180<b<360 deg), and/or had higher than average numbers of bright QSOs and CSC targets. The list of field centres can be found within the target_selection repository.
cartons.selection: Select all objects from SDSS DR16 QSO catalog that have 19.1<<tt>sdss_psfmag_i</tt><21.0 AB, and that lie within 1.49 degrees of at least one AQMES-wide field location.

cartons.name: bhm_aqmes_bonus_core
cartons.summary: Spectroscopically confirmed optically bright SDSS QSOs, selected from the SDSS QSO catalog (DR16Q, Lyke et al., 2020). Located anywhere within the SDSS DR16Q footprint.
cartons.selection: Select all objects from SDSS DR16 QSO catalog that have 16.0<<tt>sdss_psfmag_i</tt><19.1 AB

cartons.name: bhm_aqmes_bonus_faint
cartons.summary: Spectroscopically confirmed optically faint SDSS QSOs, selected from the SDSS QSO catalog (DR16Q, Lyke et al., 2020). Located anywhere within the SDSS DR16Q footprint.
cartons.selection: Select all objects from SDSS DR16 QSO catalog that have 19.1<<tt>sdss_psfmag_i</tt><21.0 AB

cartons.name: bhm_aqmes_bonus_bright
cartons.summary: Spectroscopically confirmed, extremely optically bright SDSS QSOs, selected from the SDSS QSO catalog (DR16Q, Lyke et al., 2020). Located anywhere within the SDSS DR16Q footprint.
cartons.selection: Select all objects from SDSS DR16 QSO catalog that have 14.0<<tt>sdss_psfmag_i</tt><18.0 AB

cartons.name: bhm_rm_ancillary
cartons.summary: A supporting sample of candidate QSOs which have been selected by the Gaia-unWISE AGN catalog (Shu et al., 2019) and/or the SDSS XDQSO catalog (Bovy et al., 2011). These targets are located within five (+1 backup) well known survey fields (SDSS-RM, COSMOS, XMM-LSS, ECDFS, CVZ-S/SEP, and ELIAS-S1).
cartons.selection: Starting from a parent catalog of optically selected objects in the RM fields (as presented by Yang and Shen, 2022), select candidate QSOs that satisfy all of the following: i) are identified via external ancillary methods (photo_bitmask & 3 != 0); ii) have 15<psfmag_i<21.5 AB (16<G<21.7 AB in the CVZ-S/SEP field); iii) do not have significant detections (>3σ) of parallax and/or proper motion in Gaia DR2; iv) are not vetoed due to results of visual inspections of recent spectroscopy; and v) do not lie in the SDSS-RM field

cartons.name: bhm_rm_core
cartons.summary: A sample of candidate QSOs selected via the methods presented by Yang and Shen (2022). These targets are located within five (+1 backup) well known survey fields (SDSS-RM, COSMOS, XMM-LSS, ECDFS, CVZ-S/SEP, and ELIAS-S1).
cartons.selection: Starting from a parent catalog of optically selected objects in the RM fields (as presented by Yang and Shen, 2022), select candidate QSOs that satisfy all of the following: i) are identified via the Skew-T algorithm (skewt_qso == 1); ii) have 17<psfmag_i<21.5 AB (16<G<21.7 AB in the CVZ-S/SEP field); iii) do not have significant detections (>3σ) of parallax and/or proper motion in Gaia DR2; iv) are not vetoed due to results of visual inspections of recent spectroscopy; vi) have detections in all of the gri bands (a Gaia detection is sufficient in the CVZ-S/SEP field);and vii) do not lie in the SDSS-RM field

cartons.name: bhm_rm_var
cartons.summary: A sample of candidate QSOs selected via their optical variability properties, as presented by Yang and Shen (2022). These targets are located within five (+1 backup) well known survey fields (SDSS-RM, COSMOS, XMM-LSS, ECDFS, CVZ-S/SEP, and ELIAS-S1).
cartons.selection: Starting from a parent catalog of optically selected objects in the RM fields (as presented by Yang and Shen, 2022), select candidate QSOs that satisfy all of the following: i) have significant variability in the DES or PanSTARRS1 multi-epoch photometry (var_sn[g]>3 and var_rms[g]>0.05); ii) have 17<psfmag_i<20.5 AB (16<G<21.7 AB in the CVZ-S/SEP field); iii) do not have significant detections (>3σ) of parallax and/or proper motion in Gaia DR2; iv) are not vetoed due to results of visual inspections of recent spectroscopy; and vi) do not lie in the SDSS-RM field

cartons.name: bhm_rm_known_spec
cartons.summary: A sample of known QSOs identified through optical spectroscopy from various projects, as collated by Yang and Shen (2022). These targets are located within five (+1 backup) well known survey fields (SDSS-RM, COSMOS, XMM-LSS, ECDFS, CVZ-S/SEP, and ELIAS-S1).
cartons.selection: Starting from a parent catalog of optically selected objects in the RM fields (as presented by Yang and Shen, 2022), select targets which satisfy all of the following: i) are flagged as having a spectroscopic identification (in the parent catalog or in the bhm_rm_tweaks table); ii) have 15<psfmag_i<21.7 AB (SDSS-RM, CDFS, ELIAS-S1 field), 16<G<21.7 Vega (CVZ-S/SEP field), 15<psfmag_i<21.5 AB (COSMOS and XMM-LSS fields); iii) have a spectroscopic redshift in the range 0.005<z<7; iv) are not vetoed due to results of visual inspections of recent spectroscopy

cartons.name: bhm_csc_apogee
cartons.summary: X-ray sources from the CSC2.0 source catalog with NIR counterparts in 2MASS PSC
cartons.selection: Starting from the parent catalog of CSC sources with optical/IR counterparts (bhm_csc_v2). Select entries satisfying the following criteria: i) NIR counterpart is from the 2MASS catalog, ii) 2MASS H-band magnitude measurement is not null and in the accepted range for SDSS-V: 7.0<H<14.0. Allocate cadence (exposure time) requests based on H-band magnitude.

cartons.name: bhm_csc_boss
cartons.summary: X-ray sources from the CSC2.0 source catalog with counterparts in Panstarrs1-DR1 or Gaia DR2.
cartons.selection: Starting from the parent catalog of CSC sources with optical/IR counterparts (bhm_csc_v2). Select entries satisfying the following criteria: i) optical counterpart is from the PanSTARRS1 or Gaia DR2 catalogs, ii) optical flux/magnitude is in the accepted range for SDSS-V: psfmag_[g,r,i,z]>13.5 AB and non-Null psfmag_i (objects with PanSTARRS1 counterparts); G,RP>13.0 Vega (Gaia DR2 counterparts). Deprioritize targets which already have good quality SDSS spectroscopy. Allocate cadence (exposure time) requests based on optical brightness (PS1 psfmag_i or Gaia G).
cartons.tables: bhm_csc_v2, panstarrs1, tic_v8, tables of existing SDSS spectroscopy

cartons.name: bhm_gua_bright
cartons.summary: A sample of optically bright candidate AGN lacking spectroscopic confirmations, derived from the parent sample presented by Shu et al. (2019), who applied a machine-learning approach to select QSO candidates from a combination of the Gaia DR2 and unWISE catalogs.
cartons.selection: Starting with the Shu et al. (2019) catalog, select targets which satisfy the following criteria: i) have a Random Forest probability of being a QSO of>0.8, ii) are in the (dereddened) magnitude range suitable for BOSS spectroscopy in bright time (Gdered>13.0 and RPdered>13.5, as well as Gdered<18.5 or RPdered<18.5, Vega mags), iii) do not have good optical spectroscopic measurements from a previous iteration of SDSS.
cartons.tables: gaia_unwise_agn, tic_v8, tables of existing SDSS spectroscopy

cartons.name: bhm_gua_dark
cartons.summary: A sample of optically faint candidate AGN lacking spectroscopic confirmations, derived from the parent sample presented by Shu et al. (2019), who applied a machine-learning approach to select QSO candidates from a combination of the Gaia DR2 and unWISE catalogs.
cartons.selection: Starting with the Shu et al. (2019) catalog, select targets which satisfy the following criteria: i) have a Random Forest probability of being a QSO of>0.8, ii) are in the (dereddened) magnitude range suitable for BOSS spectroscopy in dark time (Gdered>16.5 and RPdered>16.5, as well as Gdered<21.2 or RPdered<21.0, Vega mag), iii) do not have good optical spectroscopic measurements from a previous iteration of SDSS.
cartons.tables: gaia_unwise_agn, tic_v8, several tables of existing SDSS spectroscopy

cartons.name: bhm_colr_galaxies_lsdr8
cartons.summary: A supplementary magnitude limited sample of optically bright galaxies selected from the legacysurvey.org/dr8 optical/IR imaging catalog. Selection is based on optical morphology, lack of Gaia DR2 parallax, and several magnitude cuts.
cartons.selection: Starting from the legacy_survey_dr8 catalog (lsdr8), select entries satisfying all of the following criteria: i) lsdr8 morphological type != 'PSF', ii) zero or Null parallax in Gaia DR2, iii) dereddened z-band model mag<19.0 AB, and dereddened z-band fiber mag<19.5 AB, and 16<apparent z-band fiber mag<19.0 AB, and apparent r-band fiber mag>16.0 AB, and Gaia G>15.0 Vega, and Gaia RP>15.0 Vega;

cartons.name: bhm_spiders_agn_lsdr8
cartons.summary: This is the highest priority carton for SPIDERS AGN wide area follow up. The carton provides optical counterparts to point-like (unresolved) X-ray sources detected in early reductions of the first 6 months of eROSITA all sky survey data (eRASS:1). The sample is expected to contain a mixture of QSOs, AGN, stars and compact objects. The X-ray sources have been cross-matched by the eROSITA-DE team to legacysurvey.org/dr8 optical/IR counterparts. All targets are located in the sky hemisphere where MPE controls the data rights (approx. 180<l<360 deg). Due to the footprint of lsdr8, nearly all targets in this carton are located at high Galactic latitudes |b|>15 deg.
cartons.selection: Starting from a parent catalog of eRASS:1 point source → legacysurvey.org/dr8 associations (method: NWAY assisted by optical/IR priors computed via a pre-trained Random Forest, building on Salvato et al., 2022), select targets which meet all of the following criteria: i) have eROSITA detection likelihood>6.0, ii) have an X-ray → optical/IR cross-match probability (NWAY) of p_any>0.1, iii) have 13.5<fibertotmag_r<22.5 or 13.5<fibertotmag_z<21.0, iv) are not saturated in LegacySurvey imaging, v) have at least one observation in r-band and at least one observation in g- or z-band, vi) if detected by Gaia DR2 then have G>13.5 and RP>13.5 Vega. We deprioritise targets if any of the following criteria are met: a) the target already has existing good quality SDSS spectroscopy, b) the X-ray detection likelihood is <8.0, c) the target is a secondary X-ray→optical/IR association. We assign cadences (exposure time requests) based on optical brightness.
cartons.tables: erosita_superset_agn, legacy_survey_dr8, several tables of existing SDSS spectroscopy including sdss_dr16_specobj

cartons.name: bhm_spiders_agn_ps1dr2
cartons.summary: This is the second highest priority carton for SPIDERS AGN wide area follow up, it is included to expand the survey footprint beyond legacysurvey/dr8. The carton provides optical counterparts to point-like (unresolved) X-ray sources detected in early reductions of the first 6 months of eROSITA all sky survey data (eRASS:1). The sample is expected to contain a mixture of QSOs, AGN, stars and compact objects. The X-ray sources have been cross-matched by the eROSITA-DE team, first to CatWISE2020 mid-IR sources, and then to optical counterparts from the PanSTARRS1 dr2 catalog. All targets are located in the sky hemisphere where MPE controls the data rights (approx. 180<l<360 deg), and at Dec>-30 deg, spanning a wide range of Galactic latitudes. Targets at low Galactic latitudes |b|<15 deg do not drive survey strategy.
cartons.selection: Starting from a parent catalog of eRASS:1 point source → CatWISE2020 → Pan-STARRS1 associations (method: NWAY assisted by IR priors computed via a pre-trained Random Forest, building on Salvato et al., 2022), select targets which meet all of the following criteria: i) have eROSITA detection likelihood>6.0, ii) have an X-ray→IR cross-match probability of p_any>0.1, iii) have psfmag_g, psfmag_r, psfmag_i, psfmag_z>13.5 AB and at least one of psfmag_g<22.5, psfmag_r<22.0, psfmag_i<21.5 or psfmag_z<20.5, iv) are not associated with a bad Pan-STARRS1-dr2 image stack, v) have non-null measurements of psfmag_g, psfmag_r and psfmag_i, vi) if detected by Gaia DR2 then have G>13.5 and RP>13.5 Vega. We deprioritise targets if any of the following criteria are met: a) the target already has existing good quality SDSS spectroscopy, b) the X-ray detection likelihood is <8.0, c) the target is a secondary X-ray→IR association. We assign cadences (exposure time requests) based on optical brightness.
cartons.tables: erosita_superset_agn, panstarrs1, several tables of existing SDSS spectroscopy including sdss_dr16_specobj

cartons.name: bhm_spiders_agn_gaiadr2
cartons.summary: This is the third highest priority carton for SPIDERS AGN wide area follow up, it is included to expand the survey footprint to the full hemisphere where X-ray sources are available (beyond legacysurvey/dr8 and Pan-STARRS1). The carton provides optical counterparts to point-like (unresolved) X-ray sources detected in early reductions of the first 6 months of eROSITA all sky survey data (eRASS:1). The sample is expected to contain a mixture of QSOs, AGN, stars and compact objects. The X-ray sources have been cross-matched by the eROSITA-DE team, first to CatWISE2020 mid-IR sources, and then to optical counterparts from the Gaia DR2 catalog. All targets are located in the sky hemisphere where MPE controls the data rights (approx. 180<l<360 deg). The targets in this carton are distributed over a wide range of Galactic latitudes, but targets at low Galactic latitudes |b|<15 deg do not drive survey strategy.
cartons.selection: Starting from a parent catalog of eRASS:1 point source → CatWISE2020 → Gaia DR2 associations (method: NWAY assisted by IR priors computed via a pre-trained Random Forest, building on Salvato et al., 2022), select targets which meet all of the following criteria: i) have eROSITA detection likelihood>6.0, ii) have an X-ray→IR cross-match probability of p_any>0.1, iii) have G>13.5 and RP>13.5 Vega. We deprioritise targets if any of the following criteria are met: a) the target already has existing good quality SDSS spectroscopy, b) the X-ray detection likelihood is <8.0, c) the target is a secondary X-ray→IR association. We assign cadences (exposure time requests) based on optical brightness.
cartons.tables: erosita_superset_agn, tic_v8, several tables of existing SDSS spectroscopy including sdss_dr16_specobj

cartons.name: bhm_spiders_agn_skymapperdr2
cartons.summary: This is a lower ranked carton for SPIDERS AGN wide area follow up, it supplements the survey in areas which rely on Gaia DR2 (beyond LegacySurvey DR8 and Pan-STARRS1) by recovering extended targets (galaxies) that are missed by Gaia. The carton provides optical counterparts to point-like (unresolved) X-ray sources detected in early reductions of the first 6 months of eROSITA all sky survey data (eRASS:1). The sample is expected to contain a mixture of QSOs, AGN, stars and compact objects. The X-ray sources have been cross-matched by the eROSITA-DE team, first to CatWISE2020 mid-IR sources, and then to optical counterparts from the SkyMapper-dr2 catalog. All targets are located in the sky hemisphere where MPE controls the data rights (approx. 180<l<360 deg) and at Dec<0deg, spanning a wide range of Galactic latitudes. Targets at low Galactic latitudes |b|<15 deg do not drive survey strategy.
cartons.selection: Starting from a parent catalog of eRASS:1 point source → CatWISE2020 → SkyMapper-dr2 associations (method: NWAY assisted by IR priors computed via a pre-trained Random Forest, building on Salvato et al., 2022), select targets which meet all of the following criteria: i) have eROSITA detection likelihood>6.0, ii) have an X-ray→IR cross-match probability of p_any>0.1, iii) have psfmag_g, psfmag_r, psfmag_i, psfmag_z>13.5 AB and at least one of psfmag_g<22.5, psfmag_r<22.0, psfmag_i<21.5 or psfmag_z<20.5, iv) Are not associated with a bad SkyMapper source detection (flags<4), v) have non-null measurements in at least one of g_psfmag, r_psfmag and i_psfmag, vi) if detected by Gaia DR2 then have G>13.5 and RP>13.5 Vega. We deprioritise targets if any of the following criteria are met: a) the target already has existing good quality SDSS spectroscopy, b) the X-ray detection likelihood is <8.0, c) the target is a secondary X-ray→IR association. We assign cadences (exposure time requests) based on optical brightness.
cartons.tables: erosita_superset_agn, skymapper_dr2, several tables of existing SDSS spectroscopy including sdss_dr16_specobj

cartons.name: bhm_spiders_agn_supercosmos
cartons.summary: This is a lower ranked carton for SPIDERS AGN wide area follow up, it supplements the survey in areas which rely on Gaia-DR2 (beyond LegacySurvey DR8 and Pan-STARRS1) by recovering extended targets (galaxies) that are missed by Gaia. The carton provides optical counterparts to point-like (unresolved) X-ray sources detected in early reductions of the first 6 months of eROSITA all sky survey data (eRASS:1). The sample is expected to contain a mixture of QSOs, AGN, stars and compact objects. The X-ray sources have been cross-matched by the eROSITA-DE team, first to CatWISE2020 mid-IR sources, and then to optical counterparts from the SuperCosmos Sky Surveys catalog (derived from scans of photographic plates). All targets are located in the sky hemisphere where MPE controls the data rights (approx. 180<l<360 deg), spanning a wide range of Galactic latitudes. Targets at low Galactic latitudes |b|<15 deg do not drive survey strategy.
cartons.selection: Starting from a parent catalog of eRASS:1 point source → CatWISE2020 → SuperCosmos associations (method: NWAY assisted by IR priors computed via a pre-trained Random Forest, building on Salvato et al., 2022), select targets which meet all of the following criteria: i) have eROSITA detection likelihood>6.0, ii) have an X-ray→IR cross-match probability of p_any>0.1, iii) have scormagb, scormagr2, scormagi>13.5 Vega and at least one of scormagb<22.5, scormagr2<22.0, or scormagi<21.5, iv) if detected by Gaia DR2 then have G>13.5 and RP>13.5 Vega. We deprioritise targets if any of the following criteria are met: a) the target already has existing good quality SDSS spectroscopy, b) the X-ray detection likelihood is <8.0, c) the target is a secondary X-ray→IR association. We assign cadences (exposure time requests) based on optical brightness.
cartons.tables: erosita_superset_agn, supercosmos, several tables of existing SDSS spectroscopy including sdss_dr16_specobj

cartons.name: bhm_spiders_agn_efeds_stragglers
cartons.summary: This is an opportunistic supplementary carton for SPIDERS AGN follow up, aiming, where fiber resources allow, to gather a small additional number of spectra for targets in the eFEDS field (which has been repeatedly surveyed in earlier iterations of SDSS). This carton provides optical counterparts to point-like (unresolved) X-ray sources detected in early reductions of the eROSITA/eFEDS performance validation field. The sample is expected to contain a mixture of QSOs, AGN, stars and compact objects. The X-ray sources have been cross-matched by the eROSITA-DE team to legacysurvey.org/dr8 optical/IR counterparts. All targets in this carton are located within the eFEDS field (approx 126<RA<146, -3<Dec<+6 deg). These targets do not drive survey strategy.
cartons.selection: Starting from a parent catalog of eFEDS point source → LegacySurvey DR8 associations (method: NWAY assisted by optical/IR priors computed via a pre-trained Random Forest, see Salvato et al., 2022), select targets which meet all of the following criteria: i) have eROSITA detection likelihood>6.0, ii) have an X-ray → optical/IR cross-match probability (NWAY) of p_any>0.1, iii) have 13.5<fibertotmag_r<22.5 or 13.5<fibertotmag_z<21.0, iv) are not saturated in legacysurvey imaging, v) have at least one observation in r-band and at least one observation in g- or z-band, vi) if detected by Gaia DR2 then have G>13.5 and RP>13.5 Vega. We deprioritise targets if any of the following criteria are met: a) the target already has existing good quality SDSS spectroscopy, b) the X-ray detection likelihood is <8.0, c) the target is a secondary X-ray→optical/IR association. We assign cadences (exposure time requests) based on optical brightness.
cartons.tables: erosita_superset_agn, legacy_survey_dr8, several tables of existing SDSS spectroscopy including sdss_dr16_specobj

cartons.name: bhm_spiders_agn_sep
cartons.summary: This special carton is dedicated to SPIDERS AGN follow up in the CVZ-S/SEP field. The carton provides optical counterparts to point-like (unresolved) X-ray sources detected in a dedicated analysis of the first 6 months of eROSITA scanning data near the SEP. The X-ray sources have been cross-matched by the eROSITA-DE team, first to CatWISE2020 mid-IR sources, and then to optical counterparts from the Gaia DR2 catalog, using additional filtering (including Gaia EDR3 astrometric info) to reduce the contamination from foreground stars located in the LMC. All targets are located within 1.5 deg of the SEP (RA,Dec = 90.0,-66.56 deg).
cartons.selection: Starting from a parent catalog of eRASS:1/SEP point source → CatWISE2020 → Gaia DR2 associations (method: NWAY assisted by IR priors computed via a pre-trained Random Forest, building on Salvato et al., 2022), select targets which meet all of the following criteria: i) have eROSITA detection likelihood>6.0, ii) have an X-ray→IR cross-match probability of p_any>0.1, iii) have G>13.5 and RP>13.5 AB. We deprioritise targets if any of the following criteria are met: a) the X-ray detection likelihood is <8.0, b) the target is a secondary X-ray→IR association. We assign cadences (exposure time requests) based on optical brightness.
cartons.tables: erosita_superset_agn, tic_v8, several tables of existing SDSS spectroscopy including sdss_dr16_specobj

cartons.name: bhm_spiders_clusters_lsdr8
cartons.summary: This is the highest priority carton for SPIDERS Clusters wide area follow up. The carton provides a list of galaxies which are candidate members of clusters selected from early reductions of the first 6 months of eROSITA all sky survey data (eRASS:1). The X-ray clusters have been associated by the eROSITA-DE team to legacysurvey.org/dr8 (lsdr8) optical/IR counterparts using the eROMAPPER red-sequence finder algorithm (Rykoff et al., 2014; Ider Chitham et al., 2020). All targets are located in the sky hemisphere where MPE controls the data rights (approx. 180<l<360 deg). Due to the footprint of lsdr8, nearly all targets in this carton are located at high Galactic latitudes |b|>15 deg.
cartons.selection: Starting from a parent catalog of eRASS:1 → legacysurvey.org/dr8 eROMAPPER cluster associations, select targets which meet all of the following criteria: i) have 13.5<fibertotmag_r<21.0 or 13.5<fibertotmag_z<20.0, ii) if detected by Gaia DR2 then have G>13.5 and RP>13.5 Vega, iii) do not have existing good quality SDSS spectroscopy. We assign a range of priorities to targets in this carton, with BCGs top ranked, followed by candidate member galaxies according their probability of membership. We assign cadences (exposure time requests) based on optical brightness.
cartons.tables: erosita_superset_clusters, legacy_survey_dr8, several tables of existing SDSS spectroscopy including sdss_dr16_specobj, bhm_efeds_veto

cartons.name: bhm_spiders_clusters_ps1dr2
cartons.summary: This is the second highest priority carton for SPIDERS Clusters wide area follow up, it is designed to expand the survey area beyond the legacysurvey/dr8 footprint. The carton provides a list of galaxies which are candidate members of clusters selected from early reductions of the first 6 months of eROSITA all sky survey data (eRASS:1). The X-ray clusters have been associated by the eROSITA-DE team to the PanSTARRS1 dr2 catalog using the eROMAPPER red-sequence finder algorithm (Rykoff et al., 2014; Ider Chitham et al., 2020). All targets are located in the sky hemisphere where MPE controls the data rights (approx. 180<l<360 deg). Nearly all targets in this carton are located at high Galactic latitudes |b|>15 deg.
cartons.selection: Starting from a parent catalog of eRASS:1 → Pan-STARRS1-dr2 eROMAPPER cluster associations, select targets which meet all of the following criteria: i) have <tt>psfmag_r</tt>, <tt>psfmag_i</tt>, <tt>psfmag_z</tt>>13.5 AB and at least one of <tt>psfmag_r</tt><21.5, <tt>psfmag_i</tt><21.0 or <tt>psfmag_z</tt><20.5, ii) do not have existing good quality SDSS spectroscopy. We assign a range of priorities to targets in this carton, with BCGs top ranked, followed by candidate member galaxies according their probability of membership. We assign cadences (exposure time requests) based on optical brightness.
cartons.tables: erosita_superset_clusters, panstarrs1, several tables of existing SDSS spectroscopy including sdss_dr16_specobj, bhm_efeds_veto

cartons.name: bhm_spiders_clusters_efeds_stragglers
cartons.summary: This is an opportunistic supplementary carton for SPIDERS Clusters follow up, aiming, where fiber resources allow, to gather a small additional number of spectra for targets in the eFEDS field (which has been repeatedly surveyed in earlier iterations of SDSS). The carton provides a list of galaxies which are candidate members of clusters selected from early reductions of the eROSITA performance verification survey in the eFEDS field. The X-ray clusters have been associated by the eROSITA-DE team to legacysurvey.org/dr8 optical/IR counterparts. All targets in this carton are located within the eFEDS field (approx 126<RA<146, -3<Dec<+6 deg).
cartons.selection: Starting from a parent catalog of eFEDS → legacysurvey.org/dr8 cluster associations (eROMAPPER, Rykoff et al., 2014; Ider Chitham et al., 2020; Liu et al., 2022), select targets which meet all of the following criteria: i) have 13.5<fibertotmag_r<21.0 or 13.5<fibertotmag_z<20.0, ii) if detected by Gaia DR2 then have G>13.5 and RP>13.5 Vega, iii) do not have existing good quality SDSS spectroscopy. We assign a range of priorities to targets in this carton, with BCGs top ranked, followed by candidate member galaxies according their probability of membership. We assign cadences (exposure time requests) based on optical brightness.

cartons.name: bhm_spiders_agn-efeds
cartons.summary: A carton used during SDSS-V plate-mode observations, that contains candidate AGN targets found in the eROSITA/eFEDS X-ray survey field. This carton provides optical counterparts to point-like (unresolved) X-ray sources detected in early reductions ('c940/V2T') of the eROSITA/eFEDS performance validation field. The sample is expected to contain a mixture of QSOs, AGN, stars and compact objects. The X-ray sources have been cross-matched by the eROSITA-DE team to legacysurvey.org/dr8 optical/IR counterparts. All targets in this carton are located within the eFEDS field (approx 126<RA<146, -3<Dec<+6 deg).
cartons.selection: Starting from a parent catalog of eFEDS point source → legacysurvey.org/dr8 associations (primarily via NWAY assisted by optical/IR priors computed via a pre-trained Random Forest, see Salvato et al., 2022, supplemented by counterparts selected via a Likelihood Ratio using r-band magnitudes), select targets which meet all of the following criteria: i) have eROSITA detection likelihood>6.0, ii) have an X-ray → optical/IR cross-match probability of either p_any>0.1 (NWAY associations) or LR>0.2 (Likelihood Ratio associations), iii) have fibermag_r>16.5 and fibermag_r<22.0 or fibermag_z<21.0, and iv) did not receive high quality spectroscopy during the SDSS-IV= observations of the eFEDS field (Abdurro'uf et al., 2022). We deprioritise targets if any of the following criteria are met: a) the target already has existing good quality SDSS spectroscopy in SDSS DR16, b) the X-ray detection likelihood is <8.0, c) the target is a secondary X-ray→optical/IR association, or d) if the optical/IR counterpart was only chosen by the LR method. All targets were assigned a nominal cadence of: bhm_spiders_1x8 (8x15mins dark time).

cartons.name: bhm_spiders_clusters-efeds-ls-redmapper
cartons.summary: A carton used during SDSS-V plate-mode observations, that contains galaxy cluster targets found in the eROSITA/eFEDS X-ray survey field. The carton provides a list of galaxies which are candidate members of clusters selected from early reductions ('c940') of the eROSITA performance verification survey in the eFEDS field. The parent sample of galaxy clusters and their member galaxies have been selected via a joint analysis of X-ray and (several) optical/IR datasets using the eROMAPPER red-sequence finder algorithm (Rykoff et al., 2014; Ider Chitham et al., 2020). This particular carton relies on optical/IR data from legacysurvey.org/dr8. All targets in this carton are located within the eFEDS field (approx 126<RA<146, -3<Dec<+6 deg).
cartons.selection: Starting from a parent catalog of eFEDS → optical/IR cluster associations, select targets which meet all of the following criteria: i) are selected by eROMAPPER applied to legacysurvey.org/dr8 photometric data, ii) have eROSITA X-ray detection likelihood > 8.0, iii) have fibermag_r>16.5 and fibermag_r<21.0 or fibermag_z<20.0, iv) do not have existing good quality (SDSS or external) spectroscopy. We assign a range of priorities to targets in this carton, with BCGs top ranked, followed by candidate member galaxies according their probability of membership. All targets were assigned a nominal cadence of: bhm_spiders_1x8 (8x15mins dark time).

cartons.selection: Select all objects from SDSS DR16 QSO catalog that have 16.0<<tt>sdss_psfmag_i</tt><19.1 AB, that lie within 1.49 degrees of at least one AQMES-medium field location.

cartons.name: bhm_rm_ancillary
cartons.summary: A supporting sample of candidate QSOs which have been selected by the Gaia-unWISE AGN catalog (Shu et al., 2019) and/or the SDSS XDQSO catalog (Bovy et al., 2011). These targets are located within five (+1 backup) well known survey fields (SDSS-RM, COSMOS, XMM-LSS, ECDFS, CVZ-S/SEP, and ELIAS-S1).
cartons.selection: Starting from a parent catalog of optically selected objects in the RM fields (as presented by Yang and Shen, 2022), select candidate QSOs that satisfy all of the following: i) are identified via external ancillary methods (photo_bitmask & 3 != 0); ii) have 15<psfmag_i<21.5 AB; iii) do not have significant detections (>3σ) of parallax and/or proper motion in Gaia DR2; iv) are not classified as a STAR in SDSS DR16 spectroscopy; and v) do not lie in the SDSS-RM field

cartons.name: bhm_rm_core
cartons.summary: A sample of candidate QSOs selected via the methods presented by Yang and Shen (2022). These targets are located within five (+1 backup) well known survey fields (SDSS-RM, COSMOS, XMM-LSS, ECDFS, CVZ-S/SEP, and ELIAS-S1).
cartons.selection: Starting from a parent catalog of optically selected objects in the RM fields (as presented by Yang and Shen, 2022), select candidate QSOs that satisfy all of the following: i) are identified via the Skew-T algorithm (skewt_qso == 1, and skewt_qso_prior == 1 for targets in the CVZ-S/SEP field); ii) have 17<psfmag_i<21.5 AB; iii) do not have significant detections (>3σ) of parallax and/or proper motion in Gaia DR2; iv) are not classified as a STAR in SDSS DR16 spectroscopy; vi) have detections in all of the gri bands (a Gaia detection is sufficient in the CVZ-S/SEP field); and vii) do not lie in the SDSS-RM field

cartons.name: bhm_rm_var

cartons.selection: Starting from a parent catalog of optically selected objects in the RM fields (as presented by Yang and Shen, 2022), select candidate QSOs that satisfy all of the following: i) have significant variability in the DES or PanSTARRS1 multi-epoch photometry (var_sn[g]>3 and var_rms[g]>0.05); ii) have 17<psfmag_i<20.5 AB; iii) do not have significant detections (>3σ) of parallax and/or proper motion in Gaia DR2; iv) are not classified as a STAR in SDSS DR16 spectroscopy; and vi) do not lie in the SDSS-RM field

cartons.selection: Starting from a parent catalog of optically selected objects in the RM fields (as presented by Yang and Shen, 2022), select targets which satisfy all of the following: i) are flagged as having a spectroscopic identification in the parent catalog; ii) have 15<psfmag_i<21.7 AB (SDSS-RM, CDFS, ELIAS-S1, CVZ-S/SEP fields), or 15<psfmag_i<21.5 AB (COSMOS and XMM-LSS fields); iii) have a spectroscopic redshift in the range 0.005<z<7; iv) are not classified as a STAR in SDSS DR16 spectroscopy;

cartons.name: bhm_csc_boss
cartons.summary: X-ray sources from the CSC2.1 source catalog with counterparts in LegacySurvey DR10, Panstarrs1-DR1 or Gaia DR3.
cartons.selection: Starting from the parent catalog of CSC sources with optical/IR counterparts (bhm_csc_v3). Select entries satisfying the following criteria: i) optical counterpart is from the LegacySurvey DR10, PanSTARRS1 or Gaia DR3 catalogs, ii) optical flux/magnitude is in the accepted range for SDSS-V: all fibermag_[g,r,i,z] >14.5 and at least one fibermag_[g,r,i,z] <21.0 AB (objects with LegacySurvey DR10 counterparts); all psfmag_[g,r,i,z] > 14.0 and at least one psfmag_[g,r,i,z] < 22.0 AB (objects with PanSTARRS1 counterparts); 14.0 < G<20.0 Vega (Gaia DR3 counterparts). De-prioritize targets which already have good quality SDSS spectroscopy. Allocate cadence (exposure time) requests based on optical brightness (LegacySurvey fibermag_r, PS1 psfmag_i or Gaia G).

cartons.name: bhm_csc_apogee
cartons.summary: X-ray sources from the CSC2.1 source catalog with NIR counterparts in 2MASS PSC
cartons.selection: Starting from the parent catalog of CSC sources with optical/IR counterparts (bhm_csc_v2). Select entries satisfying the following criteria: i) NIR counterpart is from the 2MASS catalog, ii) 2MASS H-band magnitude measurement is not null and is within a reasonable range for SDSS-V: 7.0<H<14.0. Allocate cadence (exposure time) requests based on H-band magnitude.

cartons.name: bhm_gua_dark
cartons.summary: A sample of optically faint candidate AGN lacking spectroscopic confirmations, derived from the parent sample presented by Shu et al. (2019), who applied a machine-learning approach to select QSO candidates from a combination of the Gaia DR2 and unWISE catalogs.
cartons.selection: Starting with the Shu et al. (2019) catalog, select targets which satisfy the following criteria: i) have a Random Forest probability of being a QSO of>0.8, ii) are in the magnitude range suitable for BOSS spectroscopy in dark time (G>16.5 and RP>16.5, as well as G<21.2 or RP<21.0, Vega mag), iii) do not yet have good SDSS optical spectroscopic measurements. Note that the selection criteria are based on apparent magnitudes, rather than the dereddened magnitudes that were used in an earlier iteration of this carton.

cartons.name: bhm_gua_bright
cartons.summary: A sample of optically bright candidate AGN lacking spectroscopic confirmations, derived from the parent sample presented by Shu et al. (2019), who applied a machine-learning approach to select QSO candidates from a combination of the Gaia DR2 and unWISE catalogs.
cartons.selection: Starting with the Shu et al. (2019) catalog, select targets which satisfy the following criteria: i) have a Random Forest probability of being a QSO of>0.8, ii) are in the magnitude range suitable for BOSS spectroscopy in bright time (G>13.0 and RP>13.5, as well as G<18.5 or RP<18.5, Vega mag), iii) do not yet have good SDSS optical spectroscopic measurements. Note that the selection criteria are based on apparent magnitudes, rather than the dereddened magnitudes that were used in an earlier iteration of this carton.

cartons.name: bhm_spiders_clusters_lsdr10
cartons.summary: This is the main carton for SPIDERS galaxy cluster wide area follow up. The carton provides a catalog of galaxies which are candidate members of clusters selected from early reductions of the first 24-months of eROSITA all sky survey data (eRASS:4). The X-ray clusters have been associated by the eROSITA-DE team to legacysurvey.org/dr10 optical/IR counterparts using the eROMAPPER red-sequence finder algorithm (Rykoff et al., 2014; Ider Chitham et al., 2020). All targets are located in the sky hemisphere where MPE controls the data rights (approx. 180<l<360 deg). Targets in this carton are located at high Galactic latitudes |b|>20 deg.
cartons.selection: Starting from a parent catalog of eRASS:4 → legacysurvey.org/dr10 eROMAPPER cluster associations, select targets which meet all of the following criteria: i) have 13.5<fibertotmag_i<21.0 or 13.5<fibertotmag_z<20.5 AB, ii) if detected by Gaia DR3 then have G>13.5 and RP>13.5 Vega, and iii) do not have existing good quality SDSS spectroscopy. We assign a range of priorities to targets in this carton, with Brightest Cluster Galaxies (BCGs) top ranked, followed by candidate member galaxies according their probability of membership and whether they are within the 'core' 16<fibertotmag_i<20.5 or 16<fibertotmag_z<20.0 AB). We assign cadences (exposure time requests) based on optical brightness.

cartons.name: bhm_spiders_agn_lsdr10
cartons.summary: This is the highest priority carton for SPIDERS AGN wide area follow up. The carton provides optical counterparts to point-like (unresolved) X-ray sources detected in early reductions of the first 18 months of eROSITA all sky survey data (eRASS:3). The sample is expected to contain a mixture of QSOs, AGN, stars and compact objects. The X-ray sources have been cross-matched by the eROSITA-DE team to legacysurvey.org/DR10 optical/IR counterparts (supplemented with the DR9 catalog at Dec>+32.375 deg). All targets are located in the sky hemisphere where MPE controls the data rights (approx. 180<l<360 deg). Due to the LegacySurvey footprint, nearly all targets in this carton are located at high Galactic latitudes |b|>15 deg.
cartons.selection: Starting from a parent catalog of eRASS:3 point source → legacysurvey.org/DR10(+DR9) associations (method: NWAY assisted by optical/IR priors computed via a pre-trained Random Forest, building on Salvato et al., 2022), select targets which meet all of the following criteria: i) have eROSITA detection likelihood>6.0, ii) have an X-ray → optical/IR cross-match probability (NWAY) of p_any>0.1, iii) have 13.5<fibermag_r<22.5 or 13.5<fibermag_i<22.3 or 13.5<fibermag_z<21.5, iv) are not saturated in legacysurvey imaging, v) if detected by Gaia DR2/DR3 then have G>13.5 and RP>13.5 Vega. We deprioritise targets if any of the following criteria are met: a) the target already has existing good quality SDSS spectroscopy, b) the X-ray detection likelihood is <8.0, c) the target is a secondary X-ray→optical/IR association, d) the target falls outside the core magnitude range (i.e. doesn't satisfy one of 16.5<fibermag_r<22.0 or 16.5<fibermag_i<21.8 or 16.5<fibermag_z<21.0), e) the target lies at |b|<15 deg or at Dec<-75 deg, f) the X-ray flux is <2x10-14 erg s-1 cm-2. We slightly boost the priorities of targets which have hard X-ray detections. Cadence choices (exposure time requests per target) are based on optical brightness.

cartons.name: bhm_spiders_agn_hard
cartons.summary: This small supplementary carton provides a sample of point-like (unresolved) hard-band (2.3-5 keV) X-ray sources detected in the first 6 months of eROSITA all sky survey data (eRASS:1). The X-ray sources have been cross-matched by the eROSITA-DE team to LegacySurvey DR10 optical/IR counterparts. All targets are located in the sky hemisphere where MPE controls the data rights (approx. 180<l<360 deg).
cartons.selection: Starting from a parent catalog of multi-band eRASS:1 X-ray point source → LegacySurvey DR10 associations (method: NWAY assisted by optical/IR priors computed via a pre-trained Random Forest, building on Salvato et al., 2022), select targets which meet all of the following criteria: i) have eROSITA 2.3-5keV band detection likelihood>12.0, ii) have an X-ray → optical/IR cross-match probability (NWAY) of p_any>0.01, iii) have 13.5<fibermag_r<22.5 or 13.5<fibermag_i<22.3 or 13.5<fibermag_z<21.5, iv) are not saturated in legacysurvey imaging, v) if detected by Gaia DR2/DR3 then have G>13.5 and RP>13.5 Vega. We deprioritise targets if any of the following criteria are met: a) the target already has existing good quality SDSS spectroscopy, b) the target is a secondary X-ray→optical/IR association, c) the target falls outside the core magnitude range (i.e. doesn't satisfy one of 16.5<fibermag_r<22.0 or 16.5<fibermag_i<21.8 or 16.5<fibermag_z<21.0), d) the target lies at |b|<10 deg or at Dec<-75 deg. Cadence choices (exposure time requests per target) are based on optical brightness.

cartons.name: bhm_spiders_agn_gaiadr3

The sample is expected to contain a mixture of QSOs, AGN, stars and compact objects. The X-ray sources have been cross-matched by the eROSITA-DE team to Gaia DR3 optical counterparts. All targets are located in the sky hemisphere where MPE controls the data rights (approx. 180<l<360 deg). The targets in this carton are distributed over a wide range of Galactic latitudes, but targets at low Galactic latitudes |b|<15 deg do not drive survey strategy.
cartons.selection: Starting from a parent catalog of eRASS:3 point source → Gaia DR3 associations (method: NWAY assisted by Gaia photometric+astrometric priors computed via a pre-trained Random Forest, building on Salvato et al., 2022), select targets which meet all of the following criteria: i) have eROSITA detection likelihood>6.0, ii) have an X-ray→Gaia cross-match probability of p_any>0.1, iii) have G>13.5 and RP>13.5 Vega. We deprioritise targets if any of the following criteria are met: a) the target already has existing good quality SDSS spectroscopy, b) the X-ray detection likelihood is <8.0, c) the target is a secondary X-ray→Gaia association, d) the target falls outside the core magnitude range (i.e. doesn't satisfy 16.0<G<21.0), e) the target lies at |b|<15 deg or at Dec<-75 deg, f) the X-ray flux is <2x10-14 erg s-1 cm-2. We slightly boost the priorities of targets which have hard X-ray detections. Cadence choices (exposure time requests per target) are based on optical brightness.

cartons.name: bhm_spiders_agn_tda
cartons.summary: This is a small supplementary carton of transient/variable X-ray sources. All targets are located in the sky hemisphere where MPE controls the data rights (approx. 180<l<360 deg), and .
cartons.selection: We start from a parent catalog of transient/variable X-ray sources selected from the first N eROSITA All Sky surveys, which have been associated with LegacySurvey DR10 counterparts (method: NWAY assisted by optical/IR priors computed via a pre-trained Random Forest, building on Salvato et al., 2022). We then select targets which meet all of the following criteria: i) have 13.5<fibermag_r<22.5 or 13.5<fibermag_i<22.3 or 13.5<fibermag_z<21.5, ii) if detected by Gaia DR2/DR3 then have G>13.5 and RP>13.5 Vega. Cadence choices (exposure time requests per target) are based on optical brightness.

cartons.name: bhm_spiders_agn_sep

The X-ray sources have been cross-matched by the eROSITA-DE team to Gaia DR3 optical counterparts.
cartons.selection: Starting from a parent catalog of eRASS:5 point source → Gaia DR3 associations, select targets which meet all of the following criteria: i) have eROSITA detection likelihood>6.0, ii) have an X-ray→Gaia cross-match probability of p_any>0.1, iii) have G>13.5 and RP>13.5 Vega. We deprioritise targets if any of the following criteria are met: a) the target already has existing good quality SDSS spectroscopy, b) the X-ray detection likelihood is <8.0, c) the target is a secondary X-ray→Gaia association, d) the target falls outside the core magnitude range (i.e. doesn't satisfy 16.0<G<21.5), e) the X-ray flux is <1x10-14 erg s-1 cm-2. cartons.name: bhm_aqmes_med
cartons.summary: Spectroscopically confirmed optically bright SDSS QSOs, selected from the SDSS QSO catalog (DR16Q, Lyke et al., 2020). Located in 36 mostly disjoint fields within the SDSS QSO footprint that were pre-selected to contain higher than average numbers of bright QSOs and CSC targets. The list of field centres can be found within the target_selection repository.
cartons.selection: Select all objects from SDSS DR16 QSO catalog that have 16.0<<tt>sdss_psfmag_i</tt><19.1 AB, that lie within 1.49 degrees of at least one AQMES-medium field location.

cartons.name: bhm_aqmes_wide2
cartons.summary: Spectroscopically confirmed optically bright SDSS QSOs, selected from the SDSS QSO catalog (DR16Q, Lyke et al., 2020). Located in 425 fields within the SDSS QSO footprint, where the choice of survey area prioritized field that overlapped with the SPIDERS footprint (approx 180<b<360 deg), and/or had higher than average numbers of bright QSOs and CSC targets. The list of field centres can be found within the target_selection repository. The targets in this carton request two epochs of SDSS-V spectroscopy.
cartons.selection: Select all objects from SDSS DR16 QSO catalog that have 16.0<<tt>sdss_psfmag_i</tt><19.1 AB, and that lie within 1.49 degrees of at least one AQMES-wide field location.

cartons.name: bhm_aqmes_bonus_core
cartons.summary: Spectroscopically confirmed optically bright SDSS QSOs, selected from the SDSS QSO catalog (DR16Q, Lyke et al., 2020). Located anywhere within the SDSS DR16Q footprint.

cartons.selection: Select all objects from SDSS DR16 QSO catalog that have 14.0<<tt>sdss_psfmag_i</tt><18.0 AB

cartons.name: bhm_aqmes_bonus_faint
cartons.summary: Spectroscopically confirmed optically faint SDSS QSOs, selected from the SDSS QSO catalog (DR16Q, Lyke et al., 2020). Located anywhere within the SDSS DR16Q footprint.
cartons.selection: Select all objects from SDSS DR16 QSO catalog that have 19.1<<tt>sdss_psfmag_i</tt><21.0 AB

cartons.name: bhm_colr_galaxies_lsdr10
cartons.summary: A supplementary magnitude limited sample of optically bright galaxies selected from the LegacySurvey DR10 optical/IR imaging catalog (supplemented with the LegacySurvey DR9 catalog at Dec>+32.375 deg). Selection is based on optical morphology, lack of Gaia parallax, and several magnitude cuts.
cartons.selection: Starting from the legacy_survey_dr10 catalog (lsdr10), select entries satisfying all of the following criteria: i) lsdr10 morphological type != 'PSF', ii) lsdr10 shape_r>1.0 arcsec, iii) no detection of parallax by Gaia, iv) dereddened z-band model mag<19.0 AB, and dereddened z-band fiber mag<19.5 AB, v) within all the following apparent fiber mag limits: 16<g<22.5  and 16<r<21.5  and 16<z<20.0 AB, vi) if detected in Gaia, then G>15.0  and RP>15.0 Vega, vii) at moderate to high Galactic latitude |b|>15 deg.

cartons.name: openfibertargets_bhm_racsradio_boss
cartons.summary: Optically bright counterparts to bright 888 MHz radio sources identified by in the ASKAP/RACS-low survey (Hale et al., 2021).
cartons.selection: Subset of radio sources from the RACS-low ASKAP survey (entire sky region between declination -80 deg and +30 deg), with radio (888 MHz) flux exceeding 3 mJy and optical counterparts (with 14.5<r<20.5) identified in the footprints of LegacySurvey DR10-South using NWAY (adopting separation, positional errors, number density and a prior based on various features).

cartons.name: openfibertargets_bhm_varagn_boss
cartons.summary: Optical variability selected AGN candidates. We have used custom made forced photometry in ZTF difference images to build accurate light curves of complicated objects such as low redshift AGN, where the variable AGN point source is contaminated by the host galaxy in a PSF dependent way. With these light curves and complementary color information we built a random forest algorithm to classify all objects, obtaining 350k AGN candidates, some of which fall outside the usual locus of AGN in color-color diagrams.
cartons.selection: All ZTF forced-photometry based AGN candidates that are not included in the Gaia-unWISE color-selected AGN candidate catalog of Shu et al. (2019) and that: a) have -30<Dec<15 and |b|>20 deg, have zero proper motion according to Gaia (at 2 sigma), are not in the labeled set of well-characterized objects used in Sanchez-Saez et al. (2023), and are classified as either low-z AGN, mid-z AGN or Blazar by our classifier. For candidates classified as high-z AGN the same limits apply except that in this case |b|>30 deg and the Gaia proper motion must be within 1 sigma of zero.

cartons.name: openfibertargets_bhm_morexray_boss
cartons.summary: Counterparts of X-ray sources identified by Swift-XRT and XMM-Newton
cartons.selection: Select sources from ExSeSS (Swift, Delaney et al., 2023) and 4XMM-DR13 (Webb et al., 2020), in any X-ray band. Cross-match to LegacySurvey DR10 counterparts (using NWAY informed by WISE W1-W2 colors, with p_any>0.3), and then apply magnitude cuts (14.5<r<20 AB for bright time and 20<r<21 AB for dark time)

cartons.name: openfibertargets_bhm_hightderates_psb_boss
cartons.summary: Galaxies that have been photometrically identified as or post-starburst (E+A). Such galaxies are known to have an elevated rate of tidal disruption events of stars by their supermassive black holes.
cartons.selection: All or post-starburst galaxies identified by the machine learning algorithm in French & Zabludoff (2018)

cartons.name: openfibertargets_bhm_hightderates_qbs_boss
cartons.summary: Galaxies that have been photometrically identified as quiescent Balmer-strong (QBS). Such galaxies are known to have an elevated rate of tidal disruption events of stars by their supermassive black holes.
cartons.selection: All quiescent Balmer-strong galaxies identified by the machine learning algorithm in French & Zabludoff (2018)

cartons.name: openfibertargets_bhm_quaia_boss
cartons.summary: Quasar candidates from the Quaia Catalog (the Gaia–unWISE Quasar Catalog) that have not previously been spectroscopically observed by any generation of SDSS
cartons.selection: All Quaia G<20.5 quasars not previously spectroscopically observed by SDSS

cartons.name: openfibertargets_bhm_lofarradio_boss
cartons.summary: Optically bright counterparts of low-frequency radio sources identified by LOFAR Two Metre Sky Survey (LOTSS DR2, Hardcastle et al., 2023)
cartons.selection: Extract LOFAR radio detected sources from the LOTSS survey with published matches to LegacySurvey DR8 counterparts (Hardcastle et al., 2023). Apply a radio flux cut (Total flux>2 mJy), and apply magnitude limits (14.5<r<20.0)

cartons.summary: A sample of optically faint candidate AGN lacking spectroscopic confirmations, derived from the parent sample presented by Shu et al. (2019), who applied a machine-learning approach to select QSO candidates from a combination of the Gaia DR2 and unWISE catalogs.
cartons.selection: Starting with the Shu et al. (2019) catalog, select targets which satisfy the following criteria: i) have a Random Forest probability of being a QSO of>0.8, ii) are in the magnitude range suitable for BOSS spectroscopy in dark time (G>16.5 and RP>16.5, as well as G<21.2 or RP<21.0, Vega mag), iii) do not yet have good SDSS optical spectroscopic measurements. Note that the selection criteria are based on apparent magnitudes, rather than the dereddened magnitudes that were used in an earlier iteration of this carton. The '_d3' variation of this carton provides a shallower cadence option for targets in this sample.

cartons.name: bhm_spiders_agn_hard_d3
cartons.summary: This carton provides optical counterparts to point-like (unresolved) hard-band (2.3-5 keV) X-ray sources detected in the first 6 months of eROSITA all sky survey data (eRASS:1). The X-ray sources have been cross-matched by the eROSITA-DE team to LegacySurvey DR10 optical/IR counterparts. All targets are located in the sky hemisphere where MPE controls the data rights (approx. 180<l<360 deg).
cartons.selection: Starting from a parent catalog of multi-band eRASS:1 X-ray point source → LegacySurvey DR10 associations (method: NWAY assisted by optical/IR priors computed via a pre-trained Random Forest, building on Salvato et al., 2022), select targets which meet all of the following criteria: i) have eROSITA 2.3-5keV band detection likelihood>12.0, ii) have an X-ray → optical/IR cross-match probability (NWAY) of p_any>0.01, iii) have 13.5<fibermag_r<22.5 or 13.5<fibermag_i<22.3 or 13.5<fibermag_z<21.5, iv) are not saturated in legacysurvey imaging, v) if detected by Gaia DR2/DR3 then have G>13.5 and RP>13.5 Vega. We deprioritise targets if any of the following criteria are met: a) the target already has existing good quality SDSS spectroscopy, b) the target is a secondary X-ray→optical/IR association, c) the target falls outside the core magnitude range (i.e. doesn't satisfy one of 16.5<fibermag_r<22.0 or 16.5<fibermag_i<21.8 or 16.5<fibermag_z<21.0), d) the target lies at |b|<10 deg or at Dec<-75 deg. Cadence choices (exposure time requests per target) are based on optical brightness. The '_d3' variation of this carton provides a shallower cadence option for targets in this sample.

cartons.name: bhm_spiders_agn_lsdr10_d3
cartons.summary: This is the highest priority carton for SPIDERS AGN wide area follow up. The carton provides optical counterparts to point-like (unresolved) X-ray sources detected in early reductions of the first 18 months of eROSITA all sky survey data (eRASS:3). The sample is expected to contain a mixture of QSOs, AGN, stars and compact objects. The X-ray sources have been cross-matched by the eROSITA-DE team to legacysurvey.org/DR10 optical/IR counterparts (supplemented with the DR9 catalog at Dec>+32.375 deg). All targets are located in the sky hemisphere where MPE controls the data rights (approx. 180<l<360 deg). Due to the LegacySurvey footprint, nearly all targets in this carton are located at high Galactic latitudes |b|>15 deg.
cartons.selection: Starting from a parent catalog of eRASS:3 point source → legacysurvey.org/DR10(+DR9) associations (method: NWAY assisted by optical/IR priors computed via a pre-trained Random Forest, building on Salvato et al., 2022), select targets which meet all of the following criteria: i) have eROSITA detection likelihood>6.0, ii) have an X-ray → optical/IR cross-match probability (NWAY) of p_any>0.1, iii) have 13.5<fibermag_r<22.5 or 13.5<fibermag_i<22.3 or 13.5<fibermag_z<21.5, iv) are not saturated in legacysurvey imaging, v) if detected by Gaia DR2/DR3 then have G>13.5 and RP>13.5 Vega. We deprioritise targets if any of the following criteria are met: a) the target already has existing good quality SDSS spectroscopy, b) the X-ray detection likelihood is <8.0, c) the target is a secondary X-ray→optical/IR association, d) the target falls outside the core magnitude range (i.e. doesn't satisfy one of 16.5<fibermag_r<22.0 or 16.5<fibermag_i<21.8 or 16.5<fibermag_z<21.0), e) the target lies at |b|<15 deg or at Dec<-75 deg, f) the X-ray flux is <2x10-14 erg s-1 cm-2. We slightly boost the priorities of targets which have hard X-ray detections. The '_d3' variation of this carton provides a shallower cadence option for targets toward the optically faint end of the sample.

cartons.name: bhm_colr_galaxies_lsdr10_d3
cartons.summary: A supplementary magnitude limited sample of optically bright galaxies selected from the LegacySurvey DR10 optical/IR imaging catalog (supplemented with the LegacySurvey DR9 catalog at Dec>+32.375 deg). Selection is based on optical morphology, lack of Gaia parallax, and several magnitude cuts.
cartons.selection: Starting from the legacy_survey_dr10 catalog (lsdr10), select entries satisfying all of the following criteria: i) lsdr10 morphological type != 'PSF', ii) lsdr10 shape_r>1.0 arcsec, iii) no detection of parallax by Gaia, iv) dereddened z-band model mag<19.0 AB, and dereddened z-band fiber mag<19.5 AB, v) within all the following apparent fiber mag limits: 16<g<22.5  and 16<r<21.5  and 16<z<20.0 AB, vi) if detected in Gaia, then G>15.0  and RP>15.0 Vega, vii) at moderate to high Galactic latitude |b|>15 deg. The '_d3' variation of this carton provides a shallower cadence option for targets toward the optically faint end of the sample.

cartons.name: bhm_rm_core
cartons.summary: A sample of candidate QSOs selected via the methods presented by Yang and Shen (2022). These targets are located within five (+1 backup) well known survey fields (SDSS-RM, COSMOS, XMM-LSS, ECDFS, CVZ-S/SEP, and ELIAS-S1).
cartons.selection: Starting from a parent catalog of optically selected objects in the RM fields (as presented by Yang and Shen, 2022), select candidate QSOs that satisfy all of the following: i) are identified via the Skew-T algorithm (skewt_qso == 1); ii) have 17<psfmag_i<21.5 AB (16<G<21.7 AB in the CVZ-S/SEP field); iii) do not have significant detections (>3σ) of parallax and/or proper motion in Gaia DR2; iv) are not vetoed due to results of visual inspections of recent spectroscopy; vi) have detections in all of the gri bands (a Gaia detection is sufficient in the CVZ-S/SEP field);and vii) do not lie in the SDSS-RM field. This carton version starts with an updated parent catalog, and fixes an issue which could have caused a few targets to be unintentionally excluded.

cartons.name: bhm_rm_known_spec
cartons.summary: A sample of known QSOs identified through optical spectroscopy from various projects, as collated by Yang and Shen (2022). These targets are located within five (+1 backup) well known survey fields (SDSS-RM, COSMOS, XMM-LSS, ECDFS, CVZ-S/SEP, and ELIAS-S1).
cartons.selection: Starting from a parent catalog of optically selected objects in the RM fields (as presented by Yang and Shen, 2022), select targets which satisfy all of the following: i) are flagged as having a spectroscopic identification (in the parent catalog); ii) have 15<psfmag_i<21.7 AB (SDSS-RM, CDFS, ELIAS-S1 field), 16<G<21.7 Vega (CVZ-S/SEP field), 15<psfmag_i<21.5 AB (COSMOS and XMM-LSS fields); iii) have a spectroscopic redshift in the range 0.005<z<7; iv) are not vetoed due to results of visual inspections of recent spectroscopy. This carton version starts with an updated parent catalog, and fixes an issue which could have caused a few targets to be unintentionally excluded.

cartons.name: bhm_rm_var
cartons.summary: A sample of candidate QSOs selected via their optical variability properties, as presented by Yang and Shen (2022). These targets are located within five (+1 backup) well known survey fields (SDSS-RM, COSMOS, XMM-LSS, ECDFS, CVZ-S/SEP, and ELIAS-S1).
cartons.selection: Starting from a parent catalog of optically selected objects in the RM fields (as presented by Yang and Shen, 2022), select candidate QSOs that satisfy all of the following: i) have significant variability in the DES or PanSTARRS1 multi-epoch photometry (var_sn[g]>3 and var_rms[g]>0.05); ii) have 17<psfmag_i<20.5 AB (16<G<21.7 AB in the CVZ-S/SEP field); iii) do not have significant detections (>3σ) of parallax and/or proper motion in Gaia DR2; iv) are not vetoed due to results of visual inspections of recent spectroscopy; and vi) do not lie in the SDSS-RM field. This carton version starts with an updated parent catalog, and fixes an issue which could have caused a few targets to be unintentionally excluded.

cartons.name: bhm_rm_ancillary
cartons.summary: A supporting sample of candidate QSOs which have been selected by the Gaia-unWISE AGN catalog (Shu et al., 2019) and/or the SDSS XDQSO catalog (Bovy et al., 2011). These targets are located within five (+1 backup) well known survey fields (SDSS-RM, COSMOS, XMM-LSS, ECDFS, CVZ-S/SEP, and ELIAS-S1).
cartons.selection: Starting from a parent catalog of optically selected objects in the RM fields (as presented by Yang and Shen, 2022), select candidate QSOs that satisfy all of the following: i) are identified via external ancillary methods (photo_bitmask & 3 != 0); ii) have 15<psfmag_i<21.5 AB (16<G<21.7 AB in the CVZ-S/SEP field); iii) do not have significant detections (>3σ) of parallax and/or proper motion in Gaia DR2; iv) are not vetoed due to results of visual inspections of recent spectroscopy; and v) do not lie in the SDSS-RM field. This carton version starts with an updated parent catalog, and fixes an issue which could have caused a few targets to be unintentionally excluded.

cartons.name: manual_bhm_spiders_comm_lco
cartons.summary: A utility sample of X-ray selected targets located in the XMM-XXL-N and Stripe-82X fields having reliable spectroscopic redshifts. This carton was designed to support SDSS-V commissioning (of FPS at LCO), as a reference sample to test the accuracy and completeness of pipeline-derived redshifts extracted from new SDSS-V spectroscopic data.
cartons.selection: Starting from a compilation of X-ray selected sources in the XMM-XXL-N and Stripe-82X fields with optical counterparts in the LegacySurvey DR8 catalog, select those having i) reliable spectroscopic redshifts (confirmed visually) from previous SDSS observations,  and ii) which lie in the magnitude range 16<<tt>mag_r</tt><23.0 AB.
